description: Canonical engineering-debt register. Status is repository truth, not planning opinion: every entry names a read-only verifier that must agree with whether the debt is resolved.

verification_contract.rule: A resolved entry must have a passing named verifier; every other status must have a verifier that still reports the desired state as unmet.

debts.title: API write capability gate
debts.current_state: State-changing API operations require an explicit local non-network LocalOperatorContext through WriteCapabilityDependency; the default app is fail-closed while read and validation-only operations remain available.
debts.desired_state: State-changing operations require an explicit local non-network write capability while preserving read-only access.
debts.risk_if_ignored: A future route could bypass the local operator boundary and expose an unauthenticated state mutation.
debts.next_action: Keep the operation-level route inventory and fail-closed gate test current when routes change.

debts.title: Paper-validation legacy isolation and removal boundary
debts.current_state: Paper-validation routes are legacy, deprecated, response-marked, and write-gated. A machine-verifiable consumer manifest (SEC-02A), AST-level transitive import boundary (SEC-02B), and broker-registry runtime isolation spy test (SEC-02C) close all three threat-model gaps (TM-PV-001/002/003). The canonical verifier imports and exercises the audit module directly, the import graph reports zero forbidden transitive findings, and the golden-path API test proves zero broker-registry calls.
debts.desired_state: The threat model and executable boundary tests prohibit real adapters, credentials, and live graduation through the legacy surface; deletion criteria identify consumers and historical-read obligations without expanding the legacy API.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Future contributors may extend a legacy compatibility chain into a second execution system or treat paper-shaped objects as a broker integration scaffold.
debts.next_action: Keep the consumer manifest, import boundary, and registry isolation tests current when routes or paper modules change.

debts.title: Receipt-backed write registry
debts.current_state: The current machine-checkable write registry inventories canonical and legacy receipt-backed writes, imports registered functions, and verifies route/function coverage and execution substrate semantics.
debts.desired_state: Every receipt-backed write path is registered with its stale guard, receipt builder, index behavior, cleanup behavior, and execution substrate.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Unregistered write paths can drift in stale guards, hash binding, cleanup, or ReceiptIndex behavior.
debts.next_action: Keep registry coverage tests mandatory and update entries in the same slice as every new or removed write path.
non_goals: extracting a shared receipt command base

debts.title: Taskfile check layering
debts.current_state: check:fast owns lint, typecheck, and unit tests; check:ci composes check:fast with integration, frontend, governance, and rules; check:research composes check:ci with experiments and eval smoke; check aliases check:ci.
debts.desired_state: Named fast, CI, and research layers expose cost and dependency boundaries while the documented default preserves the intended merge gate.
debts.risk_if_ignored: The standard signal becomes slower and more brittle, encouraging contributors to skip checks or misread research-tool failures as core regressions.
debts.next_action: Keep check:ci as the documented merge gate; audit new checks for appropriate layer placement.

debts.title: Dependency grouping and dead dependency review
introduced_by: Research, Agent, eval, and trading-analysis dependency accumulation
debts.current_state: 26 retained dependencies are grouped by consumer evidence: base(9), data(5), research(5), agent(2), eval(1), dev(4). Three unused direct dependencies were removed. The base probe imports the real FinHarness core API without optional wheels; data and research profiles import their maintained consumers; the Agent runtime profile explicitly composes data + research + agent; a CI matrix rebuilds every profile from the lockfile.
debts.desired_state: Runtime-critical dependencies are separated from optional data, research, Agent, evaluation, paper, and security groups after an import/task audit proves their consumers.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Install time, vulnerability surface, resolution conflicts, and ownership ambiguity grow with each capability slice.
debts.next_action: Keep consumer manifest, group assignments, and install probes current when adding or removing dependencies.

debts.title: StateCore model split
introduced_by: Multiple bounded contexts accumulated in statecore/models.py
debts.current_state: Nine personal-finance models are extracted to personal_finance_models.py, shared primitives live in model_base.py, the extracted module no longer imports the compatibility models surface, and models.py retains identity-preserving re-exports.
debts.desired_state: Models are split by bounded context while models.py retains compatibility re-exports and metadata/OpenAPI/schema behavior remains stable.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Schema ownership, migration review, and fixture isolation become harder as unrelated bounded contexts continue sharing one module.
debts.next_action: Keep re-exports current; future extraction of higher-coupling models requires separate architectural decisions.

debts.title: Frontend action shell and module split
introduced_by: Incremental cockpit tab and write-form additions
debts.current_state: The structural verifier confirms file ownership, script order, and named ReviewActionShell call sites. The jsdom runtime fixture exists, but the debt register does not yet consume its exact-main CI result, so structural evidence cannot close the runtime claim.
debts.desired_state: Governed write forms share one action-shell contract, and API/state/view modules are separated without changing vanilla-JS behavior or jsdom compatibility.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Future cockpit and Agent-task surfaces will amplify global-state coupling and allow governance UX to drift between forms.
debts.next_action: Keep ReviewActionShell as the canonical write pattern and bind the named jsdom destructive fixture to its #386 main_commit CI identity before resolving this runtime debt.

debts.title: Toolchain Node and Rust policy
debts.current_state: mise and CI workflows use Node 22 (same major); security CI no longer installs Rust (no repository consumer found).
debts.desired_state: Local and CI Node majors agree, and every CI toolchain install has a documented repository consumer or is removed.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Local/CI divergence causes false signals, while unexplained toolchains increase CI time and maintenance ambiguity.
debts.next_action: Keep toolchain alignment check current when adding new CI toolchain dependencies.

debts.title: Execution Kernel abstraction inventory alignment
debts.current_state: The abstraction inventory classifies all nine canonical Execution Kernel facts, services, enforced capabilities, adapter boundary, simulated adapter, routes, and legacy bridge. Legacy ActionIntent/PaperValidation entries point to current bridge/delete paths instead of nonexistent future execution routes.
debts.desired_state: Canonical execution models, services, routes, adapter, and legacy bridge are classified; legacy ActionIntent/PaperValidation artifacts point to the already-existing kernel and deletion conditions.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Architecture review can classify new work against a pre-migration map and accidentally extend legacy objects or duplicate canonical execution concepts.
debts.next_action: Keep inventory changes in the same slice as canonical system additions/removals and preserve no-new-callers semantics for legacy execution-shaped artifacts.

debts.title: Execution capability enforcement
debts.current_state: The structural verifier confirms default capability values and named guard call sites. Runtime denial/no-effect fixtures exist, but the debt register does not yet consume their exact-main CI result, so source presence cannot close the runtime claim.
debts.desired_state: Every execution command is fail-closed at the service boundary when its capability is disabled; simulated submission remains enabled by explicit default and live submission/credential management remain impossible.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Capability documentation can diverge from runtime behavior, and future adapters may bypass the intended control plane through direct service calls.
debts.next_action: Bind the named denial/no-effect fixture to its #386 main_commit CI identity before resolving this runtime debt. Any real adapter, credential, or live-submit capability still requires a separate C3 design and threat-model update.
